commands.name: COPY
commands.summary: Copies the value of a key to a new key.

commands.name: DEL
commands.summary: Deletes one or more keys.

commands.name: DUMP
commands.summary: Returns a serialized representation of the value stored at a key.

commands.name: EXISTS
commands.summary: Determines whether one or more keys exist.

commands.name: EXPIRE
commands.summary: Sets the expiration time of a key in seconds.

commands.name: EXPIREAT
commands.summary: Sets the expiration time of a key to a Unix timestamp.

commands.name: EXPIRETIME
commands.summary: Returns the expiration time of a key as a Unix timestamp.

commands.name: KEYS
commands.summary: Returns all key names that match a pattern.

commands.name: MIGRATE
commands.summary: Atomically transfers a key from one Redis instance to another.

commands.name: MOVE
commands.summary: Moves a key to another database.

commands.name: OBJECT
commands.summary: A container for object introspection commands.

commands.name: OBJECT ENCODING
commands.summary: Returns the internal encoding of a Redis object.

commands.name: OBJECT FREQ
commands.summary: Returns the logarithmic access frequency counter of a Redis object.

commands.name: OBJECT HELP
commands.summary: Returns helpful text about the different subcommands.

commands.name: OBJECT IDLETIME
commands.summary: Returns the time since the last access to a Redis object.

commands.name: OBJECT REFCOUNT
commands.summary: Returns the reference count of a value of a key.

commands.name: PERSIST
commands.summary: Removes the expiration time of a key.

commands.name: PEXPIRE
commands.summary: Sets the expiration time of a key in milliseconds.

commands.name: PEXPIREAT
commands.summary: Sets the expiration time of a key to a Unix milliseconds timestamp.

commands.name: PEXPIRETIME
commands.summary: Returns the expiration time of a key as a Unix milliseconds timestamp.

commands.name: PTTL
commands.summary: Returns the expiration time in milliseconds of a key.

commands.name: RANDOMKEY
commands.summary: Returns a random key name from the database.

commands.name: RENAME
commands.summary: Renames a key and overwrites the destination.

commands.name: RENAMENX
commands.summary: Renames a key only when the target key name doesn't exist.

commands.name: RESTORE
commands.summary: Creates a key from the serialized representation of a value.

commands.name: SCAN
commands.summary: Iterates over the key names in the database.

commands.name: SORT
commands.summary: Sorts the elements in a list, a set, or a sorted set, optionally storing the result.

commands.name: SORT_RO
commands.summary: Returns the sorted elements of a list, a set, or a sorted set.

commands.name: TOUCH
commands.summary: Returns the number of existing keys out of those specified after updating the time they were last accessed.

commands.name: TTL
commands.summary: Returns the expiration time in seconds of a key.

commands.name: TYPE
commands.summary: Determines the type of value stored at a key.

commands.name: UNLINK
commands.summary: Asynchronously deletes one or more keys.

commands.name: WAIT
commands.summary: Blocks until the asynchronous replication of all preceding write commands sent by the connection is completed.

commands.name: WAITAOF
commands.summary: Blocks until all of the preceding write commands sent by the connection are written to the append-only file of the master and/or replicas.

commands.name: APPEND
commands.summary: Appends a string to the value of a key. Creates the key if it doesn't exist.

commands.name: DECR
commands.summary: Decrements the integer value of a key by one. Uses 0 as initial value if the key doesn't exist.

commands.name: DECRBY
commands.summary: Decrements a number from the integer value of a key. Uses 0 as initial value if the key doesn't exist.

commands.name: GET
commands.summary: Returns the string value of a key.

commands.name: GETDEL
commands.summary: Returns the string value of a key after deleting the key.

commands.name: GETEX
commands.summary: Returns the string value of a key after setting its expiration time.

commands.name: GETRANGE
commands.summary: Returns a substring of the string stored at a key.

commands.name: GETSET
commands.summary: Returns the previous string value of a key after setting it to a new value.

commands.name: INCR
commands.summary: Increments the integer value of a key by one. Uses 0 as initial value if the key doesn't exist.

commands.name: INCRBY
commands.summary: Increments the integer value of a key by a number. Uses 0 as initial value if the key doesn't exist.

commands.name: INCRBYFLOAT
commands.summary: Increment the floating point value of a key by a number. Uses 0 as initial value if the key doesn't exist.

commands.name: LCS
commands.summary: Finds the longest common substring.

commands.name: MGET
commands.summary: Atomically returns the string values of one or more keys.

commands.name: MSET
commands.summary: Atomically creates or modifies the string values of one or more keys.

commands.name: MSETNX
commands.summary: Atomically modifies the string values of one or more keys only when all keys don't exist.

commands.name: PSETEX
commands.summary: Sets both string value and expiration time in milliseconds of a key. The key is created if it doesn't exist.

commands.name: SET
commands.summary: Sets the string value of a key, ignoring its type. The key is created if it doesn't exist.

commands.name: SETEX
commands.summary: Sets the string value and expiration time of a key. Creates the key if it doesn't exist.

commands.name: SETNX
commands.summary: Set the string value of a key only when the key doesn't exist.

commands.name: SETRANGE
commands.summary: Overwrites a part of a string value with another by an offset. Creates the key if it doesn't exist.

commands.name: STRLEN
commands.summary: Returns the length of a string value.

commands.name: SUBSTR
commands.summary: Returns a substring from a string value.

commands.name: HDEL
commands.summary: Deletes one or more fields and their values from a hash. Deletes the hash if no fields remain.

commands.name: HEXISTS
commands.summary: Determines whether a field exists in a hash.

commands.name: HGET
commands.summary: Returns the value of a field in a hash.

commands.name: HGETALL
commands.summary: Returns all fields and values in a hash.

commands.name: HINCRBY
commands.summary: Increments the integer value of a field in a hash by a number. Uses 0 as initial value if the field doesn't exist.

commands.name: HINCRBYFLOAT
commands.summary: Increments the floating point value of a field by a number. Uses 0 as initial value if the field doesn't exist.

commands.name: HKEYS
commands.summary: Returns all fields in a hash.

commands.name: HLEN
commands.summary: Returns the number of fields in a hash.

commands.name: HMGET
commands.summary: Returns the values of all fields in a hash.

commands.name: HMSET
commands.summary: Sets the values of multiple fields.

commands.name: HRANDFIELD
commands.summary: Returns one or more random fields from a hash.

commands.name: HSCAN
commands.summary: Iterates over fields and values of a hash.

commands.name: HSET
commands.summary: Creates or modifies the value of a field in a hash.

commands.name: HSETNX
commands.summary: Sets the value of a field in a hash only when the field doesn't exist.

commands.name: HSTRLEN
commands.summary: Returns the length of the value of a field.

commands.name: HVALS
commands.summary: Returns all values in a hash.

commands.name: BLMOVE
commands.summary: Pops an element from a list, pushes it to another list and returns it. Blocks until an element is available otherwise. Deletes the list if the last element was moved.

commands.name: BLMPOP
commands.summary: Pops the first element from one of multiple lists. Blocks until an element is available otherwise. Deletes the list if the last element was popped.

commands.name: BLPOP
commands.summary: Removes and returns the first element in a list. Blocks until an element is available otherwise. Deletes the list if the last element was popped.

commands.name: BRPOP
commands.summary: Removes and returns the last element in a list. Blocks until an element is available otherwise. Deletes the list if the last element was popped.

commands.name: BRPOPLPUSH
commands.summary: Pops an element from a list, pushes it to another list and returns it. Block until an element is available otherwise. Deletes the list if the last element was popped.

commands.name: LINDEX
commands.summary: Returns an element from a list by its index.

commands.name: LINSERT
commands.summary: Inserts an element before or after another element in a list.

commands.name: LLEN
commands.summary: Returns the length of a list.

commands.name: LMOVE
commands.summary: Returns an element after popping it from one list and pushing it to another. Deletes the list if the last element was moved.

commands.name: LMPOP
commands.summary: Returns multiple elements from a list after removing them. Deletes the list if the last element was popped.

commands.name: LPOP
commands.summary: Returns the first elements in a list after removing it. Deletes the list if the last element was popped.

commands.name: LPOS
commands.summary: Returns the index of matching elements in a list.

commands.name: LPUSH
commands.summary: Prepends one or more elements to a list. Creates the key if it doesn't exist.

commands.name: LPUSHX
commands.summary: Prepends one or more elements to a list only when the list exists.

commands.name: LRANGE
commands.summary: Returns a range of elements from a list.

commands.name: LREM
commands.summary: Removes elements from a list. Deletes the list if the last element was removed.

commands.name: LSET
commands.summary: Sets the value of an element in a list by its index.

commands.name: LTRIM
commands.summary: Removes elements from both ends a list. Deletes the list if all elements were trimmed.

commands.name: RPOP
commands.summary: Returns and removes the last elements of a list. Deletes the list if the last element was popped.

commands.name: RPOPLPUSH
commands.summary: Returns the last element of a list after removing and pushing it to another list. Deletes the list if the last element was popped.

commands.name: RPUSH
commands.summary: Appends one or more elements to a list. Creates the key if it doesn't exist.

commands.name: RPUSHX
commands.summary: Appends an element to a list only when the list exists.

commands.name: SADD
commands.summary: Adds one or more members to a set. Creates the key if it doesn't exist.

commands.name: SCARD
commands.summary: Returns the number of members in a set.

commands.name: SDIFF
commands.summary: Returns the difference of multiple sets.

commands.name: SDIFFSTORE
commands.summary: Stores the difference of multiple sets in a key.

commands.name: SINTER
commands.summary: Returns the intersect of multiple sets.

commands.name: SINTERCARD
commands.summary: Returns the number of members of the intersect of multiple sets.

commands.name: SINTERSTORE
commands.summary: Stores the intersect of multiple sets in a key.

commands.name: SISMEMBER
commands.summary: Determines whether a member belongs to a set.

commands.name: SMEMBERS
commands.summary: Returns all members of a set.

commands.name: SMISMEMBER
commands.summary: Determines whether multiple members belong to a set.

commands.name: SMOVE
commands.summary: Moves a member from one set to another.

commands.name: SPOP
commands.summary: Returns one or more random members from a set after removing them. Deletes the set if the last member was popped.

commands.name: SRANDMEMBER
commands.summary: Get one or multiple random members from a set

commands.name: SREM
commands.summary: Removes one or more members from a set. Deletes the set if the last member was removed.

commands.name: SSCAN
commands.summary: Iterates over members of a set.

commands.name: SUNION
commands.summary: Returns the union of multiple sets.

commands.name: SUNIONSTORE
commands.summary: Stores the union of multiple sets in a key.

commands.name: BZMPOP
commands.summary: Removes and returns a member by score from one or more sorted sets. Blocks until a member is available otherwise. Deletes the sorted set if the last element was popped.

commands.name: BZPOPMAX
commands.summary: Removes and returns the member with the highest score from one or more sorted sets. Blocks until a member available otherwise.  Deletes the sorted set if the last element was popped.

commands.name: BZPOPMIN
commands.summary: Removes and returns the member with the lowest score from one or more sorted sets. Blocks until a member is available otherwise. Deletes the sorted set if the last element was popped.

commands.name: ZADD
commands.summary: Adds one or more members to a sorted set, or updates their scores. Creates the key if it doesn't exist.

commands.name: ZCARD
commands.summary: Returns the number of members in a sorted set.

commands.name: ZCOUNT
commands.summary: Returns the count of members in a sorted set that have scores within a range.

commands.name: ZDIFF
commands.summary: Returns the difference between multiple sorted sets.

commands.name: ZDIFFSTORE
commands.summary: Stores the difference of multiple sorted sets in a key.

commands.name: ZINCRBY
commands.summary: Increments the score of a member in a sorted set.

commands.name: ZINTER
commands.summary: Returns the intersect of multiple sorted sets.

commands.name: ZINTERCARD
commands.summary: Returns the number of members of the intersect of multiple sorted sets.

commands.name: ZINTERSTORE
commands.summary: Stores the intersect of multiple sorted sets in a key.

commands.name: ZLEXCOUNT
commands.summary: Returns the number of members in a sorted set within a lexicographical range.

commands.name: ZMPOP
commands.summary: Returns the highest- or lowest-scoring members from one or more sorted sets after removing them. Deletes the sorted set if the last member was popped.

commands.name: ZMSCORE
commands.summary: Returns the score of one or more members in a sorted set.

commands.name: ZPOPMAX
commands.summary: Returns the highest-scoring members from a sorted set after removing them. Deletes the sorted set if the last member was popped.

commands.name: ZPOPMIN
commands.summary: Returns the lowest-scoring members from a sorted set after removing them. Deletes the sorted set if the last member was popped.

commands.name: ZRANDMEMBER
commands.summary: Returns one or more random members from a sorted set.

commands.name: ZRANGE
commands.summary: Returns members in a sorted set within a range of indexes.

commands.name: ZRANGEBYLEX
commands.summary: Returns members in a sorted set within a lexicographical range.

commands.name: ZRANGEBYSCORE
commands.summary: Returns members in a sorted set within a range of scores.

commands.name: ZRANGESTORE
commands.summary: Stores a range of members from sorted set in a key.

commands.name: ZRANK
commands.summary: Returns the index of a member in a sorted set ordered by ascending scores.

commands.name: ZREM
commands.summary: Removes one or more members from a sorted set. Deletes the sorted set if all members were removed.

commands.name: ZREMRANGEBYLEX
commands.summary: Removes members in a sorted set within a lexicographical range. Deletes the sorted set if all members were removed.

commands.name: ZREMRANGEBYRANK
commands.summary: Removes members in a sorted set within a range of indexes. Deletes the sorted set if all members were removed.

commands.name: ZREMRANGEBYSCORE
commands.summary: Removes members in a sorted set within a range of scores. Deletes the sorted set if all members were removed.

commands.name: ZREVRANGE
commands.summary: Returns members in a sorted set within a range of indexes in reverse order.

commands.name: ZREVRANGEBYLEX
commands.summary: Returns members in a sorted set within a lexicographical range in reverse order.

commands.name: ZREVRANGEBYSCORE
commands.summary: Returns members in a sorted set within a range of scores in reverse order.

commands.name: ZREVRANK
commands.summary: Returns the index of a member in a sorted set ordered by descending scores.

commands.name: ZSCAN
commands.summary: Iterates over members and scores of a sorted set.

commands.name: ZSCORE
commands.summary: Returns the score of a member in a sorted set.

commands.name: ZUNION
commands.summary: Returns the union of multiple sorted sets.

commands.name: ZUNIONSTORE
commands.summary: Stores the union of multiple sorted sets in a key.

commands.name: XACK
commands.summary: Returns the number of messages that were successfully acknowledged by the consumer group member of a stream.

commands.name: XADD
commands.summary: Appends a new message to a stream. Creates the key if it doesn't exist.

commands.name: XAUTOCLAIM
commands.summary: Changes, or acquires, ownership of messages in a consumer group, as if the messages were delivered to as consumer group member.

commands.name: XCLAIM
commands.summary: Changes, or acquires, ownership of a message in a consumer group, as if the message was delivered a consumer group member.

commands.name: XDEL
commands.summary: Returns the number of messages after removing them from a stream.

commands.name: XGROUP
commands.summary: A container for consumer groups commands.

commands.name: XGROUP CREATE
commands.summary: Creates a consumer group.

commands.name: XGROUP CREATECONSUMER
commands.summary: Creates a consumer in a consumer group.

commands.name: XGROUP DELCONSUMER
commands.summary: Deletes a consumer from a consumer group.

commands.name: XGROUP DESTROY
commands.summary: Destroys a consumer group.

commands.name: XGROUP HELP
commands.summary: Returns helpful text about the different subcommands.

commands.name: XGROUP SETID
commands.summary: Sets the last-delivered ID of a consumer group.

commands.name: XINFO
commands.summary: A container for stream introspection commands.

commands.name: XINFO CONSUMERS
commands.summary: Returns a list of the consumers in a consumer group.

commands.name: XINFO GROUPS
commands.summary: Returns a list of the consumer groups of a stream.

commands.name: XINFO HELP
commands.summary: Returns helpful text about the different subcommands.

commands.name: XINFO STREAM
commands.summary: Returns information about a stream.

commands.name: XLEN
commands.summary: Return the number of messages in a stream.

commands.name: XPENDING
commands.summary: Returns the information and entries from a stream consumer group's pending entries list.

commands.name: XRANGE
commands.summary: Returns the messages from a stream within a range of IDs.

commands.name: XREAD
commands.summary: Returns messages from multiple streams with IDs greater than the ones requested. Blocks until a message is available otherwise.

commands.name: XREADGROUP
commands.summary: Returns new or historical messages from a stream for a consumer in a group. Blocks until a message is available otherwise.

commands.name: XREVRANGE
commands.summary: Returns the messages from a stream within a range of IDs in reverse order.

commands.name: XSETID
commands.summary: An internal command for replicating stream values.

commands.name: XTRIM
commands.summary: Deletes messages from the beginning of a stream.

commands.name: PFADD
commands.summary: Adds elements to a HyperLogLog key. Creates the key if it doesn't exist.

commands.name: PFCOUNT
commands.summary: Returns the approximated cardinality of the set(s) observed by the HyperLogLog key(s).

commands.name: PFDEBUG
commands.summary: Internal commands for debugging HyperLogLog values.

commands.name: PFMERGE
commands.summary: Merges one or more HyperLogLog values into a single key.

commands.name: PFSELFTEST
commands.summary: An internal command for testing HyperLogLog values.

commands.name: GEOADD
commands.summary: Adds one or more members to a geospatial index. The key is created if it doesn't exist.

commands.name: GEODIST
commands.summary: Returns the distance between two members of a geospatial index.

commands.name: GEOHASH
commands.summary: Returns members from a geospatial index as geohash strings.

commands.name: GEOPOS
commands.summary: Returns the longitude and latitude of members from a geospatial index.

commands.name: GEORADIUS
commands.summary: Queries a geospatial index for members within a distance from a coordinate, optionally stores the result.

commands.name: GEORADIUSBYMEMBER
commands.summary: Queries a geospatial index for members within a distance from a member, optionally stores the result.

commands.name: GEORADIUSBYMEMBER_RO
commands.summary: Returns members from a geospatial index that are within a distance from a member.

commands.name: GEORADIUS_RO
commands.summary: Returns members from a geospatial index that are within a distance from a coordinate.

commands.name: GEOSEARCH
commands.summary: Queries a geospatial index for members inside an area of a box or a circle.

commands.name: GEOSEARCHSTORE
commands.summary: Queries a geospatial index for members inside an area of a box or a circle, optionally stores the result.

commands.name: BITCOUNT
commands.summary: Counts the number of set bits (population counting) in a string.

commands.name: BITFIELD
commands.summary: Performs arbitrary bitfield integer operations on strings.

commands.name: BITFIELD_RO
commands.summary: Performs arbitrary read-only bitfield integer operations on strings.

commands.name: BITOP
commands.summary: Performs bitwise operations on multiple strings, and stores the result.

commands.name: BITPOS
commands.summary: Finds the first set (1) or clear (0) bit in a string.

commands.name: GETBIT
commands.summary: Returns a bit value by offset.

commands.name: SETBIT
commands.summary: Sets or clears the bit at offset of the string value. Creates the key if it doesn't exist.

commands.name: ASKING
commands.summary: Signals that a cluster client is following an -ASK redirect.

commands.name: CLUSTER
commands.summary: A container for Redis Cluster commands.

commands.name: CLUSTER ADDSLOTS
commands.summary: Assigns new hash slots to a node.

commands.name: CLUSTER ADDSLOTSRANGE
commands.summary: Assigns new hash slot ranges to a node.

commands.name: CLUSTER BUMPEPOCH
commands.summary: Advances the cluster config epoch.

commands.name: CLUSTER COUNT-FAILURE-REPORTS
commands.summary: Returns the number of active failure reports active for a node.

commands.name: CLUSTER COUNTKEYSINSLOT
commands.summary: Returns the number of keys in a hash slot.

commands.name: CLUSTER DELSLOTS
commands.summary: Sets hash slots as unbound for a node.

commands.name: CLUSTER DELSLOTSRANGE
commands.summary: Sets hash slot ranges as unbound for a node.

commands.name: CLUSTER FAILOVER
commands.summary: Forces a replica to perform a manual failover of its master.

commands.name: CLUSTER FLUSHSLOTS
commands.summary: Deletes all slots information from a node.

commands.name: CLUSTER FORGET
commands.summary: Removes a node from the nodes table.

commands.name: CLUSTER GETKEYSINSLOT
commands.summary: Returns the key names in a hash slot.

commands.name: CLUSTER HELP
commands.summary: Returns helpful text about the different subcommands.

commands.name: CLUSTER INFO
commands.summary: Returns information about the state of a node.

commands.name: CLUSTER KEYSLOT
commands.summary: Returns the hash slot for a key.

commands.name: CLUSTER LINKS
commands.summary: Returns a list of all TCP links to and from peer nodes.

commands.name: CLUSTER MEET
commands.summary: Forces a node to handshake with another node.

commands.name: CLUSTER MYID
commands.summary: Returns the ID of a node.

commands.name: CLUSTER MYSHARDID
commands.summary: Returns the shard ID of a node.

commands.name: CLUSTER NODES
commands.summary: Returns the cluster configuration for a node.

commands.name: CLUSTER REPLICAS
commands.summary: Lists the replica nodes of a master node.

commands.name: CLUSTER REPLICATE
commands.summary: Configure a node as replica of a master node.

commands.name: CLUSTER RESET
commands.summary: Resets a node.

commands.name: CLUSTER SAVECONFIG
commands.summary: Forces a node to save the cluster configuration to disk.

commands.name: CLUSTER SET-CONFIG-EPOCH
commands.summary: Sets the configuration epoch for a new node.

commands.name: CLUSTER SETSLOT
commands.summary: Binds a hash slot to a node.

commands.name: CLUSTER SHARDS
commands.summary: Returns the mapping of cluster slots to shards.

commands.name: CLUSTER SLAVES
commands.summary: Lists the replica nodes of a master node.

commands.name: CLUSTER SLOTS
commands.summary: Returns the mapping of cluster slots to nodes.

commands.name: READONLY
commands.summary: Enables read-only queries for a connection to a Redis Cluster replica node.

commands.name: READWRITE
commands.summary: Enables read-write queries for a connection to a Reids Cluster replica node.

commands.name: AUTH
commands.summary: Authenticates the connection.

commands.name: CLIENT
commands.summary: A container for client connection commands.

commands.name: CLIENT CACHING
commands.summary: Instructs the server whether to track the keys in the next request.

commands.name: CLIENT GETNAME
commands.summary: Returns the name of the connection.

commands.name: CLIENT GETREDIR
commands.summary: Returns the client ID to which the connection's tracking notifications are redirected.

commands.name: CLIENT HELP
commands.summary: Returns helpful text about the different subcommands.

commands.name: CLIENT ID
commands.summary: Returns the unique client ID of the connection.

commands.name: CLIENT INFO
commands.summary: Returns information about the connection.

commands.name: CLIENT KILL
commands.summary: Terminates open connections.

commands.name: CLIENT LIST
commands.summary: Lists open connections.

commands.name: CLIENT NO-EVICT
commands.summary: Sets the client eviction mode of the connection.

commands.name: CLIENT NO-TOUCH
commands.summary: Controls whether commands sent by the client affect the LRU/LFU of accessed keys.

commands.name: CLIENT PAUSE
commands.summary: Suspends commands processing.

commands.name: CLIENT REPLY
commands.summary: Instructs the server whether to reply to commands.

commands.name: CLIENT SETINFO
commands.summary: Sets information specific to the client or connection.

commands.name: CLIENT SETNAME
commands.summary: Sets the connection name.

commands.name: CLIENT TRACKING
commands.summary: Controls server-assisted client-side caching for the connection.

commands.name: CLIENT TRACKINGINFO
commands.summary: Returns information about server-assisted client-side caching for the connection.

commands.name: CLIENT UNBLOCK
commands.summary: Unblocks a client blocked by a blocking command from a different connection.

commands.name: CLIENT UNPAUSE
commands.summary: Resumes processing commands from paused clients.

commands.name: ECHO
commands.summary: Returns the given string.

commands.name: HELLO
commands.summary: Handshakes with the Redis server.

commands.name: PING
commands.summary: Returns the server's liveliness response.

commands.name: QUIT
commands.summary: Closes the connection.

commands.name: RESET
commands.summary: Resets the connection.

commands.name: SELECT
commands.summary: Changes the selected database.

commands.name: PSUBSCRIBE
commands.summary: Listens for messages published to channels that match one or more patterns.

commands.name: PUBLISH
commands.summary: Posts a message to a channel.

commands.name: PUBSUB
commands.summary: A container for Pub/Sub commands.

commands.name: PUBSUB CHANNELS
commands.summary: Returns the active channels.

commands.name: PUBSUB HELP
commands.summary: Returns helpful text about the different subcommands.

commands.name: PUBSUB NUMPAT
commands.summary: Returns a count of unique pattern subscriptions.

commands.name: PUBSUB NUMSUB
commands.summary: Returns a count of subscribers to channels.

commands.name: PUBSUB SHARDCHANNELS
commands.summary: Returns the active shard channels.

commands.name: PUBSUB SHARDNUMSUB
commands.summary: Returns the count of subscribers of shard channels.

commands.name: PUNSUBSCRIBE
commands.summary: Stops listening to messages published to channels that match one or more patterns.

commands.name: SPUBLISH
commands.summary: Post a message to a shard channel

commands.name: SSUBSCRIBE
commands.summary: Listens for messages published to shard channels.

commands.name: SUBSCRIBE
commands.summary: Listens for messages published to channels.

commands.name: SUNSUBSCRIBE
commands.summary: Stops listening to messages posted to shard channels.

commands.name: UNSUBSCRIBE
commands.summary: Stops listening to messages posted to channels.

commands.name: DISCARD
commands.summary: Discards a transaction.

commands.name: EXEC
commands.summary: Executes all commands in a transaction.

commands.name: MULTI
commands.summary: Starts a transaction.

commands.name: UNWATCH
commands.summary: Forgets about watched keys of a transaction.

commands.name: WATCH
commands.summary: Monitors changes to keys to determine the execution of a transaction.

commands.name: EVAL
commands.summary: Executes a server-side Lua script.

commands.name: EVALSHA
commands.summary: Executes a server-side Lua script by SHA1 digest.

commands.name: EVALSHA_RO
commands.summary: Executes a read-only server-side Lua script by SHA1 digest.

commands.name: EVAL_RO
commands.summary: Executes a read-only server-side Lua script.

commands.name: FCALL
commands.summary: Invokes a function.

commands.name: FCALL_RO
commands.summary: Invokes a read-only function.

commands.name: FUNCTION
commands.summary: A container for function commands.

commands.name: FUNCTION DELETE
commands.summary: Deletes a library and its functions.

commands.name: FUNCTION DUMP
commands.summary: Dumps all libraries into a serialized binary payload.

commands.name: FUNCTION FLUSH
commands.summary: Deletes all libraries and functions.

commands.name: FUNCTION HELP
commands.summary: Returns helpful text about the different subcommands.

commands.name: FUNCTION KILL
commands.summary: Terminates a function during execution.

commands.name: FUNCTION LIST
commands.summary: Returns information about all libraries.

commands.name: FUNCTION LOAD
commands.summary: Creates a library.

commands.name: FUNCTION RESTORE
commands.summary: Restores all libraries from a payload.

commands.name: FUNCTION STATS
commands.summary: Returns information about a function during execution.

commands.name: SCRIPT
commands.summary: A container for Lua scripts management commands.

commands.name: SCRIPT DEBUG
commands.summary: Sets the debug mode of server-side Lua scripts.

commands.name: SCRIPT EXISTS
commands.summary: Determines whether server-side Lua scripts exist in the script cache.

commands.name: SCRIPT FLUSH
commands.summary: Removes all server-side Lua scripts from the script cache.

commands.name: SCRIPT HELP
commands.summary: Returns helpful text about the different subcommands.

commands.name: SCRIPT KILL
commands.summary: Terminates a server-side Lua script during execution.

commands.name: SCRIPT LOAD
commands.summary: Loads a server-side Lua script to the script cache.

commands.name: ACL
commands.summary: A container for Access List Control commands.

commands.name: ACL CAT
commands.summary: Lists the ACL categories, or the commands inside a category.

commands.name: ACL DELUSER
commands.summary: Deletes ACL users, and terminates their connections.

commands.name: ACL DRYRUN
commands.summary: Simulates the execution of a command by a user, without executing the command.

commands.name: ACL GENPASS
commands.summary: Generates a pseudorandom, secure password that can be used to identify ACL users.

commands.name: ACL GETUSER
commands.summary: Lists the ACL rules of a user.

commands.name: ACL HELP
commands.summary: Returns helpful text about the different subcommands.

commands.name: ACL LIST
commands.summary: Dumps the effective rules in ACL file format.

commands.name: ACL LOAD
commands.summary: Reloads the rules from the configured ACL file.

commands.name: ACL LOG
commands.summary: Lists recent security events generated due to ACL rules.

commands.name: ACL SAVE
commands.summary: Saves the effective ACL rules in the configured ACL file.

commands.name: ACL SETUSER
commands.summary: Creates and modifies an ACL user and its rules.

commands.name: ACL USERS
commands.summary: Lists all ACL users.

commands.name: ACL WHOAMI
commands.summary: Returns the authenticated username of the current connection.

commands.name: BGREWRITEAOF
commands.summary: Asynchronously rewrites the append-only file to disk.

commands.name: BGSAVE
commands.summary: Asynchronously saves the database(s) to disk.

commands.name: COMMAND
commands.summary: Returns detailed information about all commands.

commands.name: COMMAND COUNT
commands.summary: Returns a count of commands.

commands.name: COMMAND DOCS
commands.summary: Returns documentary information about one, multiple or all commands.

commands.name: COMMAND GETKEYS
commands.summary: Extracts the key names from an arbitrary command.

commands.name: COMMAND GETKEYSANDFLAGS
commands.summary: Extracts the key names and access flags for an arbitrary command.

commands.name: COMMAND HELP
commands.summary: Returns helpful text about the different subcommands.

commands.name: COMMAND INFO
commands.summary: Returns information about one, multiple or all commands.

commands.name: COMMAND LIST
commands.summary: Returns a list of command names.

commands.name: CONFIG
commands.summary: A container for server configuration commands.

commands.name: CONFIG GET
commands.summary: Returns the effective values of configuration parameters.

commands.name: CONFIG HELP
commands.summary: Returns helpful text about the different subcommands.

commands.name: CONFIG RESETSTAT
commands.summary: Resets the server's statistics.

commands.name: CONFIG REWRITE
commands.summary: Persists the effective configuration to file.

commands.name: CONFIG SET
commands.summary: Sets configuration parameters in-flight.

commands.name: DBSIZE
commands.summary: Returns the number of keys in the database.

commands.name: DEBUG
commands.summary: A container for debugging commands.

commands.name: FAILOVER
commands.summary: Starts a coordinated failover from a server to one of its replicas.

commands.name: FLUSHALL
commands.summary: Removes all keys from all databases.

commands.name: FLUSHDB
commands.summary: Remove all keys from the current database.

commands.name: INFO
commands.summary: Returns information and statistics about the server.

commands.name: LASTSAVE
commands.summary: Returns the Unix timestamp of the last successful save to disk.

commands.name: LATENCY
commands.summary: A container for latency diagnostics commands.

commands.name: LATENCY DOCTOR
commands.summary: Returns a human-readable latency analysis report.

commands.name: LATENCY GRAPH
commands.summary: Returns a latency graph for an event.

commands.name: LATENCY HELP
commands.summary: Returns helpful text about the different subcommands.

commands.name: LATENCY HISTOGRAM
commands.summary: Returns the cumulative distribution of latencies of a subset or all commands.

commands.name: LATENCY HISTORY
commands.summary: Returns timestamp-latency samples for an event.

commands.name: LATENCY LATEST
commands.summary: Returns the latest latency samples for all events.

commands.name: LATENCY RESET
commands.summary: Resets the latency data for one or more events.

commands.name: LOLWUT
commands.summary: Displays computer art and the Redis version

commands.name: MEMORY
commands.summary: A container for memory diagnostics commands.

commands.name: MEMORY DOCTOR
commands.summary: Outputs a memory problems report.

commands.name: MEMORY HELP
commands.summary: Returns helpful text about the different subcommands.

commands.name: MEMORY MALLOC-STATS
commands.summary: Returns the allocator statistics.

commands.name: MEMORY PURGE
commands.summary: Asks the allocator to release memory.

commands.name: MEMORY STATS
commands.summary: Returns details about memory usage.

commands.name: MEMORY USAGE
commands.summary: Estimates the memory usage of a key.

commands.name: MODULE
commands.summary: A container for module commands.

commands.name: MODULE HELP
commands.summary: Returns helpful text about the different subcommands.

commands.name: MODULE LIST
commands.summary: Returns all loaded modules.

commands.name: MODULE LOAD
commands.summary: Loads a module.

commands.name: MODULE LOADEX
commands.summary: Loads a module using extended parameters.

commands.name: MODULE UNLOAD
commands.summary: Unloads a module.

commands.name: MONITOR
commands.summary: Listens for all requests received by the server in real-time.

commands.name: PSYNC
commands.summary: An internal command used in replication.

commands.name: REPLCONF
commands.summary: An internal command for configuring the replication stream.

commands.name: REPLICAOF
commands.summary: Configures a server as replica of another, or promotes it to a master.

commands.name: RESTORE-ASKING
commands.summary: An internal command for migrating keys in a cluster.

commands.name: ROLE
commands.summary: Returns the replication role.

commands.name: SAVE
commands.summary: Synchronously saves the database(s) to disk.

commands.name: SHUTDOWN
commands.summary: Synchronously saves the database(s) to disk and shuts down the Redis server.

commands.name: SLAVEOF
commands.summary: Sets a Redis server as a replica of another, or promotes it to being a master.

commands.name: SLOWLOG
commands.summary: A container for slow log commands.

commands.name: SLOWLOG GET
commands.summary: Returns the slow log's entries.

commands.name: SLOWLOG HELP
commands.summary: Show helpful text about the different subcommands

commands.name: SLOWLOG LEN
commands.summary: Returns the number of entries in the slow log.

commands.name: SLOWLOG RESET
commands.summary: Clears all entries from the slow log.

commands.name: SWAPDB
commands.summary: Swaps two Redis databases.

commands.name: SYNC
commands.summary: An internal command used in replication.

commands.name: TIME
commands.summary: Returns the server time.
